name: aberdeen-1996-enterprise-executives-year-2000-solution
title: How Enterprise Executives Play a Pivotal Role in the Year 2000 Solution
description: Aberdeen Group argues that the Year 2000 (Y2K) problem is fundamentally a competitive strategy issue, not merely a technical one, and that senior executives — not just IT departments — must take leadership roles. The study contends that non-Y2K-compliant enterprises will suffer competitive disadvantage as customers, partners (e.g., Wal-Mart), and regulators require proof of Y2K readiness, and prescribes a proactive executive response with trusted external assistance.
